items.title: Starmer and Macron working to try to end Russia-Ukraine war at summit | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer says Britain and France have agreed to work with Ukraine on halting hostilities in the country's three-year, full-scale war with Russia.

The announcement comes as the British leader is hosting French President Emmanuel Macron and other leaders for a summit on ending the war - and bolstering the continent's defense posture. Its importance has been magnified by the unprecedented scolding Ukrainian President Volodymr Zelenskyy received at the White House Friday.

President Donald Trump accused Zelenskyy of being ungrateful for US help. Starmer said he's focused on bridging those differences.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: How Israel's cutting off aid to Gaza will impact millions of Palestinians? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel has announced that it is stopping humanitarian aid entering Gaza. Israel has warned of consequences if Hamas refuses to accept a US proposal to continue the first phase of the truce during the Islamic month of Ramadan. The proposal calls for Hamas to release half of the remaining hostages and extend the truce through the Jewish Passover holiday on April 20th.

items.title: Britain hosts summit to end war in Ukraine - Zelenskyy demands security guarantees | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Britain is preparing to host European leaders at a summit aimed at finding a common position on ending Russia's war against Ukraine. Ahead of the meeting, Prime Minister Keir Starmer hosted Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy in Downing Street - and pledged nearly three billion dollars to buy military equipment. 
Amid Trump's threat to cut US support, European leaders are reaffirming their support for Ukraine as they navigate a new transatlantic era.

Chapters:
00:00 Britain to host summit on ending Ukraine war
03:05 Maria Martisiute​​​, Policy Analyst at the European Policy Centre
08:51 Mikhail Alexseeev, Professor of political science at San Diego State University
12:56 Sergei Satanovskii, DW Correspondent

items.title: When a Child Dies | DW Reporter
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Mia was born in October 2020, and her family enjoyed seven months of bliss. But things took a sudden turn when Sarah and Christopher's baby began experiencing seizures. She  was taken to the hospital, where Mia was diagnosed with a rare genetic disorder. She is terminally ill. The family receives support at the Berliner Herz children's hospice in Berlin. We accompanied them during the final months before Mia's passing.

[Warning: This report deals with the topics of grief and death.]


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Can Europe welcome Zelenskyy at Ukraine summit without enraging Trump? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has held talks with the British Prime Minister Keir Starmer in Downing Steet. Zelenskyy is in the UK to rally support for Ukraine after his bitter clash Donald Trump at the White House. Other European leaders will be joining a summit in the British capital on Sunday -- to discuss security in the event of any ceasefire or peace agreement between Russia and Ukraine. Starmer gave the Ukrainian leader a warm welcome to number 10 Downing Street. 

Chapters:
0:00 Zelenskyy holds talks with the British PM Starmer
02:32 DW Correspondents Teri Schulz and Nick Connolly
08:12 Gustav Gressel Austrian National Defense Academy
12:40 Kira Rudik, Holos party leader
19:09 Arseniy Yatsenyuk Former Ukrainian Prime Minister

items.title: Deadline for Gaza ceasefire phase one nears -- can phase two be implemented? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The first six-week phase of the Gaza ceasefire deal is expiring. Hamas has released dozens of hostages in exchange for Israel setting free hundreds of Palestinian prisoners. But it's not clear whether negotiators meeting in Cairo can agree on the terms for stage two.

items.title: Forceful Ukrainian and global reactions one day on, as Zelenskyy visits London | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In the wake of a fiery clash between Donald Trump, his vice-president JD Vance and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy at the White House, leaders from around the world have offered forceful reaction, with EU diplomat Kallas saying the free world needs a new leader.

items.title: Turkey: PKK militants declare ceasefire | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Kurdish PKK militants have declared a ceasefire with Turkey two days after their jailed leader asked them to lay down their arms.

Abdullah Öcalan urged fellow members of his outlawed Kurdistan Workers' Party to disarm and disband. The group, which has fought for autonomy since the 1980s, is considered a terrorist organization in the EU, US and Turkey. Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan welcomed Öcalan's overture, but warned against what he called provocations aiming to disrupt peace efforts.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.author: DW News
items.content:

items.title: Profits over safety? Airlines and plane manufacturers want to reduce the number of pilots | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Airlines and airplane manufacturers want to reduce the number of pilots flying a plane from two to one. But pilots around the world are furious. They say it’s a gamble with safety.
